clinicalSummary.oneSentence: Hepatocellular carcinoma is a primary liver malignancy that often arises in chronically injured liver tissue and may present with subtle constitutional or abdominal symptoms.
clinicalSummary.keyMechanism: Malignant transformation of hepatocytes within a chronically damaged liver creates a focal tumor whose behavior reflects both cancer biology and liver reserve.
clinicalSummary.timeScale: often evolves over months to years on top of chronic liver disease
clinicalSummary.patientExperienceSummary: Patients may notice fatigue, abdominal discomfort, weight loss, or decompensation clues rather than a single dramatic early symptom.

physiologyPrerequisites.topic: basic liver structure and blood supply
physiologyPrerequisites.whyItMatters: Readers need to understand why local vascular behavior and organ reserve matter to the story.
The detective work depends on understanding why a tumor in the liver affects markers, symptoms, and treatment choice.

pathophysiology.event: chronic liver injury creates a pro-carcinogenic environment
pathophysiology.mechanism: Inflammation, regeneration, and genomic injury increase malignant transformation risk.

pathophysiology.event: a malignant hepatocyte clone expands into a focal tumor
pathophysiology.mechanism: Autonomous growth distorts normal architecture and competes with surrounding tissue.

pathophysiology.event: tumor growth alters local vascular behavior and hepatic reserve
pathophysiology.mechanism: Expansion changes perfusion and contributes to liver dysfunction or mass-effect findings.

commonSigns: jaundice when liver dysfunction is present
historyClues: background chronic liver disease may be present
physicalExamClues: liver edge fullness can coexist with chronic liver disease stigmata
presentation.typicalTimecourse: often insidious until surveillance or progressive symptoms reveal the lesion

labs.name: liver panel
labs.purpose: characterize hepatic function and injury pattern
labs.expectedFinding: may show injury or dysfunction but is not diagnostic by itself

labs.name: alpha-fetoprotein
labs.purpose: supportive tumor marker when elevated in the right context
labs.expectedFinding: may be elevated in some cases and should be treated as a clue rather than a universal rule

imaging.name: multiphasic liver imaging
imaging.purpose: characterize lesion behavior and vascular pattern
imaging.expectedFinding: arterial enhancement with washout may support diagnosis in context

pathology.name: tumor histology
pathology.expectedFinding: malignant hepatocellular features when tissue is required

diagnosticLogic: Interpret the lesion in the setting of chronic liver disease risk.
Use imaging behavior, clinical context, and selected biomarkers together.
Avoid treating one biomarker as a stand-alone diagnosis.

differentials.whyConsidered: another primary liver malignancy can produce focal lesions and systemic symptoms
differentials.whyLessLikely: pattern of imaging behavior and hepatocellular biology can point away from biliary-origin malignancy

differentials.whyConsidered: liver lesions may reflect spread from an extrahepatic cancer
differentials.whyLessLikely: clinical context and lesion behavior may support a primary hepatocellular tumor instead

acuteStabilization: stabilize bleeding, pain, or decompensation when present
address urgent complications of liver dysfunction as clinically indicated

definitiveTherapies.name: surgical or locoregional therapy
definitiveTherapies.mechanismOfAction: remove or destroy tumor tissue in selected localized disease contexts
definitiveTherapies.whenUsed: selected early or localized disease with adequate liver reserve

definitiveTherapies.name: systemic therapy
definitiveTherapies.mechanismOfAction: target tumor survival pathways or tumor-associated angiogenesis depending on regimen

monitoring: follow liver function and treatment response
notes: management is both stage-aware and liver-function-aware

evidence.claimText: Chronic liver injury increases HCC risk.
reviewNotes: Preferred baseline source for hepatology and liver tumor evaluation.

evidence.claimText: HCC reflects malignant transformation of hepatocytes.
Tumor growth changes local perfusion and tissue architecture.
Liver tests may be abnormal in HCC but are not diagnostic by themselves.
reviewNotes: Preferred baseline source for hepatology and liver tumor evaluation.

evidence.claimText: AFP may be elevated in HCC but is not universally elevated.
reviewNotes: Preferred baseline source for hepatology and liver tumor evaluation.

evidence.claimText: Multiphasic imaging behavior helps identify HCC in the right clinical context.
reviewNotes: Use for imaging behavior and lesion characterization claims.

evidence.claimText: Histology can confirm malignant hepatocellular features when tissue is needed.
reviewNotes: Use for pathology confirmation and histology language.

evidence.claimText: Localized HCC may be treated with surgery or locoregional therapy when clinically appropriate.
reviewNotes: Preferred for stage-aware oncology treatment recommendations.

evidence.claimText: Advanced unresectable HCC may require systemic therapy tailored to disease context.
reviewNotes: Preferred for stage-aware oncology treatment recommendations.
